baseline.comparisonWorkflow: manual source research, backlog shaping, implementation planning, QA notes, and release or rollback notes without Portarium-governed execution
captureMethods: git commit and pull request timestamps
manual issue or Work Item notes

usefulOutcome.definition: A governed source-to-micro-SaaS Artifact package accepted for builder use, including cited source evidence, product or technical brief, bounded implementation or backlog Artifact, QA evidence, and release or rollback notes.
acceptanceSignals: operator accepts the Artifact package for builder use
required source claims cite Source Snapshots or user-provided references
tests, review, or QA evidence exists for implementation-bearing Artifacts
manual fallback, rollback, and stop-using-it events are recorded when they occur

trustedSources.allowedUse: implementation-affecting official docs, API references, release notes, and repository refs
implementation hypotheses, package behaviour, and community issue context with confidence boundaries
builder intent, constraints, non-goals, quality bar, and local workflow context

artifactPlan.evidenceDepth: brief objective, audience, constraints, quality bar, taste notes, non-goals, and acceptance thresholds
Source Snapshots, cited claims, freshness, confidence, claim boundaries, and open questions
problem hypothesis, target user, integration feasibility, non-goals, and acceptance criteria
bounded spec, Work Item list, code change, or handoff package linked to the dossier
focused test output, typecheck or dependency-cruiser status when feasible, review notes, and release or rollback notes
baseline and pilot metric table with acceptance rating and follow-up Beads

requiredEvidenceEvents.description: Active operator minutes spent starting, steering, reviewing, approving, fixing, and closing each Run.
An approval denial, request-changes decision, blocked Run, missing evidence signal, or policy exception.
Rollback trigger, scope, decision, recovery action, evidence refs, and completion criteria.
Failed generation, failed test, incorrect Artifact, missing source, stale source, tool error, or operator rejection.
Work moved outside Portarium because the governed path was blocked, too slow, wrong, unsafe, or not worth continuing.
A moment where the builder stopped using the workflow for a bounded piece of work, with reason and recovery route.
Accepted Artifact package, rejected package with rationale, or package carried forward into builder work.
Accept, reject, request-changes, or wrong-direction review signal with evidence refs and rationale.

actions: mark generated Artifact as rejected or superseded
restore prior branch or file state
record evidence refs and operator rationale
route remaining work as Manual-only Human Tasks
create follow-up Beads for missing Policy, evidence, or tooling
change readiness label to pilot-candidate or demo-only in the next truthful artifact update
publish a failure or concern report
restart only after the blocker has evidence-backed remediation

rollbackTriggers: unsafe Action or Policy violation escape
uncited material claim drives implementation or approval context
operator minutes increase without accepted quality or KPI tradeoff
same exception fingerprint repeats twice without remediation
builder stops using the workflow for real work during the pilot window
completionCriteria: affected Run is stopped, corrected, or explicitly moved to Manual-only
Artifact status reflects accepted, rejected, superseded, or rolled back
Evidence Log records trigger, scope, operator decision, and recovery action
follow-up Beads exist for unresolved defects before a broader claim is made

requiredTriggers: workflow becomes slower than manual path
Artifact quality is below acceptance threshold
governance blocks progress without useful safety signal
